summary: Fixed fast-cache-validation inversion: ensure_ast_cache now calls cache.validate(cached, fast=True) when fast_cache_validation=True and the payload has a manifest. Corrected the existing test FakeCache.validate fixture and added two explicit regression tests that record the fast arg passed to validate().
known_risks: Focused coverage for src/sattlint/_app_analysis_loading.py under tests/test_app_analysis_project_cache.py is 87.89%, so this slice does not yet meet a 100% file-coverage goal.

commands: COVERAGE_FILE=artifacts/tmp/.coverage.app-analysis-loading-slice scripts/run_repo_python.sh -m coverage run -m pytest --no-cov tests/test_app_analysis_project_cache.py -x -q --tb=short && COVERAGE_FILE=artifacts/tmp/.coverage.app-analysis-loading-slice scripts/run_repo_python.sh -m coverage report -m src/sattlint/_app_analysis_loading.py
notes: Focused coverage rerun: 24 passed in 0.69s; src/sattlint/_app_analysis_loading.py = 87.89% (322 statements, 39 missed), so the file did not reach 100%.
Ruff on tests/test_app_analysis_project_cache.py: all checks passed.
Pyright on tests/test_app_analysis_project_cache.py: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 informations.
The new test covers the _safe_count TypeError fallback, but this module-scoped test file still leaves unrelated lines in src/sattlint/_app_analysis_loading.py uncovered.

reviewer_notes: Only _app_analysis_loading.py was changed in production code (one character: fast=False -> fast=True on line 237).
cache.py was not modified; its validate() semantics are correct.
Two new tests prove the fast contract at the boundary.
Post-slice validation after the new _safe_count fallback test leaves the focused coverage gate short of 100% for src/sattlint/_app_analysis_loading.py.
